message:
bench(storage): cover multi-label AND filter under list_issues

The `list_issues` benchmark group exercised single-label filters and
status/priority filters but had no coverage for the multi-label AND
case (`labels: ["a", "b"]` requires BOTH labels present). That path
expands the SQL with an `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM labels WHERE ...)`
subquery per label and is materially different from the single-label
fast path, so a regression there would not show up in any existing
bench.

This commit adds:

  - `setup_db_with_multi_label_issues(count)`: builds a DB where every
    even-index issue carries an extra `label-even` label on top of
    its normal `label-{i % 10}` round-robin assignment. The shape
    deliberately produces a real multi-label intersection — roughly
    half the rows match `label-even` and a tenth match `label-2`,
    so the AND filter has to actually narrow.

  - `bench_list_issues_filtered::filtered_multi_label_and`: 1000-row
    DB, filter `["label-2", "label-even"]`, calls the same
    `multi_label_storage.list_issues(&filters)` path the CLI uses.
    Wraps the call in `black_box` and reports under the existing
    bench-group instrumentation (`log_bench_start`/`log_bench_end`)
    so the result lands in the same telemetry stream as the rest of
    the storage benches.

No production-code change; benches only. Useful as a regression
guard when the upcoming search-filter reorder commit lands on the
same query path — multi-label EXISTS chains are exactly where a
naive predicate-order rewrite would regress.
